reviews.review_title: Name of the game is wrong For what it is
reviews.review_description:
Nock is one of the most inventive VR multiplayer experiences I’ve played. The core gameplay—high-speed arena skating/flying, physics-based ball control, and chaotic team matches—feels fresh and rewarding once you understand it. As someone who’s logged serious time and whose own house and property were used as the model for the park map, I consider myself an MVP-level player. The movement system, weapon variety, and pure arcade energy make it a blast with friends or skilled randoms.
That said, the game has a major retention problem that keeps it from reaching its full potential.
The title “Bow + Arrow Soccer” sets completely wrong expectations. New players naturally approach it like real-world archery: full draw to the cheek or face, proper anchor point, controlled release. In Nock’s hyper-fast environment, this technique is a serious handicap. Arrow power and fire rate scale with maximum controller separation (essentially how far you stretch the string hand away from the bow hand). Traditional form delivers roughly half power at best and slows you down dramatically in a game that demands rapid, repeated shots.
Most newcomers spend their first few matches firing weak arrows, struggling to keep up with arrow regeneration, and wondering how veterans are launching shots so quickly and often. By game 3 or 4, many simply quit—frustrated and sometimes physically sore from repetitive improper form that can strain shoulders. I’ve tried coaching new players in matches, but you can’t reach everyone in public lobbies.
A clearer name like “Slingshot Soccer” (or something that signals the arcade physics toy nature) would help immensely. Hell. Rubber band soccer makes more sense! The current branding primes people for a simulation they won’t get. A short, mandatory tutorial tip explicitly showing the optimal “max-stretch outward” technique could fix most of the onboarding pain.
These feel like basic UX and communication issues for a live multiplayer game. Nock has real “gem” DNA—fun, physical, and unique—but the lack of polish in player guidance is holding it back. With better first impressions and retention tools, this could easily build a much larger, healthier community.
If you’re a veteran looking for a skill-ceiling multiplayer shooter with creative movement, it’s worth your time. Just be ready to help the new players you encounter.
Great game overall, but the lack of people makes this game look bad. Consider making it free to revive it.
It’s so fun but that’s the bad part it’s a great game everyone has left behind as soon as I got it it is so good people who made it save your game make it free to play

I know the hype train has long moved on for this app, but it's still one of the most fun things you can do in VR: any time you want to play low gravity ice skating archery soccer/football, this is the app to use!
The characters don’t go Lalalalalalalalalalala this time, sad
As you can see quest is full of children, this game is a hidden gem that need to be available to everyone to make it succeed. Its a ghost town right now and its sad because its so fun to play.

It used to be a great game back when it released (I gave 5 stars in my 03.2022 review), but due to some questionable decisions by developers the game is dead now, the number of online players is single digit, and developers already announced that they aren't going to update Nock any more.

Wasn’t expecting to love this one but the intuitive controls and floaty movement had me hooked instantly. It feels great to fling yourself in the air, pull back your bow and get a direct hit on a ball that’s flying past you.

They'll tell you in a review that this games the best ever! for that block with the weirdo on it. But i wonder what productive criticism they would have shared? Hmmm 


I'll take that pug block Tommy!

New review: Volleyball mode is super great! Big fan! I’ll probably try to get my friend to play as well. Highly recommend if you haven’t played before or haven’t played in a while. Albeit player count is low. But 1v1 with a friend alone is probably worth it.
The game itself  is awesome, but the lack of players make it pointless.
I like this game

I’m a week-one Nock player. When I first saw the game, I knew it was going to be a blast, and it was, for a long time. Even when I got stomped by better players, I knew I was improving, and most of the games were close. However, as the game’s life cycle progressed and the player count dropped, it became harder to improve and have those intense, close games that made the game so much fun. And that’s why it’s so painful to see the game in its current state. I’m at a loss as to how Normal let this gem of a game fade away; they truly had something special and are currently squandering its potential. It’s been almost a year since any major updates—last I recall, it was the volleyball mode, which, frankly, was a terrible idea that only further divided the already small player base.

I suspect Normal has moved on to developing a new game, but given their track record with Nock, I’m doubtful their next project will fare any better, even if it’s as good as Nock. As knowing how to maintain a game is crucial in today’s live service model, and it seems they haven’t quite grasped that.

I’m not expecting Nock to become the next Gorilla Tag overnight, but I do hope it can survive in a healthier state. To give it a fighting chance, I believe it should go free-to-play—no one’s buying it at this point anyway. The game already has in-game transactions that could continue to support it if there were players to make purchases. Therefore, Normal should advertise this change to attract both old and new players. With these steps, there’s a chance this amazing game could not only survive but even thrive. Please, Normal, I’m begging you.

reviews.review_title: the best game ever made for vr.
reviews.review_description: great fun with friends, very replayable and a (usually) great community. amazing game, thanks normal!

reviews.review_title: Someone called me the n word
reviews.review_description: I was in a match where I beat the person said only said gg th first time the second time I called him out for calling me the N word and being mean and he was rude the whole game and then all I said the rest of the game was gg and the same thing happened.

Got this awhile ago and finally gave it a try. It's horrible. No other way to say it. No tutorial or anything about HOW to play. Tried a quick game and stood there during BLANKS! No arrows, no idea how to move. Just watched the Bot basically play itself. Been playing IDU for several years.. I GET how arrow-shooting works. Just garbage ... SMH

reviews.review_title: Got it on release day 2022 still a fav
reviews.review_description: Got it on release day still love it to this day
You should of listened: Not making it free to play and just make season pass available to purchase. you doomed your own game. Saw this from a mile off. Now it’s too late!!! Great game down the 🚽

reviews.review_title: Think of it as a slingshot
reviews.review_description: And save your shoulder
Ever since i played nock i have been addicted to it, it is so fun and intense and a nice game if your a competitive type of person.

Game displayed price $10. However $10 only covers training level and matches against bots that do basically nothing. 
All levels are locked. To do anything else you have to pay another $8 - $10 for season pass which is essentially a subscription.

developer_response.body:
Hey Ryan, nothing in game aside from optional cosmetics are "locked" behind the season pass.
All the play options can be accessed by shooting "PLAY NOW" then "PLAY ONLINE" all you need is the base game.
You will have to complete 10 casual games before unlocking Competative mode, but I assure you nothing is "locked" behind the season pass.

reviews.review_description:
Look. It’s worth double the price as is, flaws, squeakers and all. 

The game is just a few bug fixes and some polish away from literally being the best game humanity has ever produced. IRL, flatscreen, VR, or otherwise. 

…. Ahhh forget it, you’ll understand the hype once you make your first outplay.

Catch you on the pitch 🤝

It's a cool and fun game but it's not beginner friendly. Me and my friend are getting destroyed by players who are more expierenced with the game. While we are still finding out the right angles, our opponents are dribbeling the ball like Messi and shooting it like Ronaldo. After the match you can see every players rank. In almost every match there is someone with a way higher rank than we are (probably because of the lack of players to find a fair match). So the game as concept is fun but the matchmaking makes it a lot less.

developer_response.body:
Hey LoyalSheep, were actually working on improving the tutorial and brainstorming ideas for training scenarios, join the community on discord to partake in training sessions throughout the week and meet some great people.  nock.game/discord
If you would like to email me any other concerns or questions about the game feel free!  towellie@normalvr.com

reviews.review_description: This is a great game and I love it I would totally recommend it but there are a few matchmaking issues but they really don’t affect gameplay

developer_response.body:
Hey, thanks so much fo taking the time to leave a review for us! I'm glad to hear you're enjoying Nock :)

The matchmaker is constantly being improved and optimized, but you may occasionally match with some users that are outside your skill range. This is so that you don't end up waiting too long for a match if no similarly ranked/skilled players are online and looking for matches. You can read a bit more about how matchmaking in the game works in our Discord: nock.game/discord.

I'd love to hear from you about how else we can improve Nock and make it a perfect experience for you!

- Nick

reviews.review_description: I love this game

reviews.review_title: I love this It’s worths every penny!
reviews.review_description: It’s going to be game of the year for sure only for $12 for premium really good VR sport this is amazing get it!

reviews.review_title: This is a steppingstone for vr esports
reviews.review_description: One of if not THE best game on the Oculus quest.

reviews.review_title: Alright prolly the best game on vr BUT
reviews.review_description: Game devs please tell me ya are working on the servers cus i been trying to grind this game but i legit only get to play 30 seconds and then get disconnected

developer_response.body:
UPDATE: Hey Towellie here, I'd love to chat with you sometime and answer any questions you might have! Drop me a message  discord.gg/nockgame   Towellie#0706  --  towellie@normalvr.com  -- Hey Akabash, thanks for taking the time to leave us a review :) 

Unfortunately, some users are running into these rare connection issues, and our team is working hard to diagnose and fix them ASAP. That being said, I'm really sorry to hear you're having trouble playing Nock.

Hit me up on our Discord: nock.game/discord or email (nick@normalvr.com) and we'll see if we can figure this out!

- Nick

reviews.review_description: This game is very fun and within an hour easy to learn.

This games is a lot of fun. The movement and controls come to you like second hand nature which creates for a great immersive experience. The gameplay is extremely fast pace. I wish there was some recommendations from the devs on how to best play the game in a given space. Because the game is so fast pace I punch the wall quite a lot. Also because of all the moving parts and the ball is flying everywhere plus the fact that the movement is sliding 100 percent of the time making a goal quite hard. Can take a while for a novice player to become decent at the game. Idk if there’s a way to hard stop and hard turn, but it should be a thing. A lot of people will like the game and be good at the game. At the same time a lot of people will download this game play the tutorial see how cool this game could for them then get into a real match with people who know how to play and lose 15 - 0 then put the game down after trying to be good at the game for couple days.   When you get a goal it’s very satisfying. 


I had to come back to my review. The first review I made was only after playing it a few hours. Now it’s been a week it feels like of having this game. And o must say the more you play the better you get. The controls are so intuitive and smooth better if not just as good as the best vr game. I recommend playing with a Matt on the floor standing up and turn and turn around using your body. It’s a lot of movements and water breaks are necessary but seeing yourself improve so satisfying. Scoring a goal is so satisfying.  DEVS IF YOU SEE THIS PLEASE IN YOUR NEXT UPDATE MAKE IT POSSIBLE TO EASILY AND QUICKLY MUTE A PLAYER WITHIN GAME. That is all. Can’t wait for new maps and skins and bows this game is awesome. We need tournaments or something idk once the community grows. This game is awesome and I’m getting everyone to download it.
The best game I’ve played yet. To much fun! ^_^

reviews.review_title: major lag (for me, maybe not others)
reviews.review_description: I had a difficult time playing because of lag. If the ball appears to move to the other side of the field within a second, then the game is unplayable. That is how my first impression with the game was. The lag would cause me to chase a ball that was no longer anywhere near where I was going, and so on and so forth. The practice levels by myself were very fun, and I would play a solo mode with pleasure.  The game works, I'm sure, under personal circumstances that provide a strong connection, constantly.

developer_response.body: UPDATE: Hey Towellie here, I'd love to chat with you sometime and answer any questions you might have! Drop me a message  discord.gg/nockgame   Towellie#0706  --  towellie@normalvr.com  -- Hey drop me a message on discord or email, lets see if we can solve this for you.    discord.gg/nockgame   Towellie#0706  |   towellie@normalvr.com

reviews.review_description: I just bought this game after seeing previews of it all week and I am hooked. It’s so fun to play, the voice chat implementation is amazing. But of course there are a few bugs because this is a new game, sometimes there’s a 75% chance that after you Nick someone out with the ball, the ball will bounce everywhere and likely end up going into a random teams goal. Not really a bug but I did find that this game drains a considerable amount of battery. Hoping the devs can work out these bugs and work on battery consumption by maybe adding a graphics setting, if they do, no doubt this game is the best on the market right now. And only for $10 this game is a 100% buy

reviews.review_description: I’ve been playing rocket league for 6 years now and this is literally the vr version of the game. Very fun would recommend
I love it

This game and it’s devs are 100% different. They understand the hustle. They want to know the community and on top of it they made one of the best games if not the best game in 2022. This is an obvious Esport material game with plenty of smooth mechanics and a wonderful avatar system animation. It’s super fun and for all ages hit when it comes to being competitive this game is endless in the possibilities of shot types and blocks and rapid fire ego shots. Instant classic and is a take to stay period because of the devs and how they are. Thanks for listening to your community and we are ready to soar with you to the Esports main stage!! Thanks again!

developer_response.body: Thank you soo much for the kind words ! It was a pleasure playing and I cant wait to face you again! We have super exciting things in the works right now that I just know you're going to love , feel free to chat any time  towellie@normalvr.com  or on discord towellie#0706   nock.game/discord !

reviews.review_description: I bought Oculus last week and this is the experience I was looking for. Definitely there are some improvements to make but the baseline is solid. Can't wait to see future updates.

Honestly if you're into competitive gaming and enjoy a challenge then this is the game to get. I got this game around two weeks after it came out and it's super addicting and a lot of fun. It still is a pretty small community so you start to recognize names and make friends. But overall an amazing game. I would highly recommend getting it.
Just incredible! Buy it now before it becomes 20 bucks or even $30! This game is amazing!

I can’t even explain it in words. The glide, the bow draw… the feeling when you land 3 of those in a row and spin around mid air to land the last shot…

I can’t get enough of this game. Runs flawlessly for me. Awesome unique art style that prioritizes performance over high poly nonsense. Spend on average 20 mins watching my  avatar dance to music because the animations are so groovy.

Its clear that the team has paid great attention to the details and listens to feedback. It shows.

10/10!
